name: Department Of Corrections
description_short: DOCCU is a member-owned credit union serving corrections department employees since 1957, offering vehicle loans, mortgages, credit cards, and deposit accounts with competitive rates.
description_long: Department Of Corrections Credit Union (DOCCU) has been operating since 1957, making it a well-established financial institution with over 65 years of service to its membership. The organization is a not-for-profit, member-owned credit union insured by the NCUA, distinguishing it from traditional for-profit banks. DOCCU offers a comprehensive range of financial products including vehicle loans with rates as low as 5.50% APR, unsecured personal loans starting at 10.50% APR, Visa credit cards with 1% cash back and rates from 8.90% APR, mortgage loans, share savings accounts, checking accounts with direct deposit, ATM and debit card access, and specialty products like vacation club accounts and overdraft protection. They also provide loan calculators, skip-a-pay options, payment protection, and various digital banking services including online and mobile banking platforms. What distinguishes DOCCU is its strong emphasis on personalized member service, as evidenced by multiple customer testimonials praising staff by name and noting 25-30+ year member relationships. The credit union emphasizes competitive lending rates, flexible loan terms, and the ability to serve members through multiple channels including phone, email, and physical branch locations (including a Homer Branch). Their vehicle loan program includes a dedicated car-finder tool, and they actively market debt consolidation solutions. The organization maintains modern digital capabilities with mobile apps and online banking while preserving the personal touch associated with credit unions. DOCCU is best suited for members within their eligible employment base (corrections department employees and families) seeking below-market rates on vehicle and personal loans with personalized service. The main caveat is that membership appears restricted to specific employee populations, making it inaccessible to the general public. Additionally, while rates are competitive, they are not extraordinary compared to other credit unions, and specific approval requirements and qualifications apply to promotional offers.
services: Vehicle loans with flexible terms and rates as low as 5.50% APR
Unsecured personal loans starting at 10.50% APR for debt consolidation and other purposes
Visa credit cards with 1% cash back, no annual fee, and tap-to-pay functionality
Mortgage loans for home purchase and refinancing
Share savings accounts and share-secured loan products
Checking accounts with direct deposit capability
ATM and Visa debit card access
Online and mobile banking platforms with click pay functionality
Vacation club savings accounts with dedicated savings tracking
Loan protection and payment protection insurance options
Overdraft protection and skip-a-pay loan payment flexibility
pros: Vehicle loan APR as low as 5.50%, competitive with or better than many credit unions and banks
Visa credit card with 1% cash back, no annual fee, and tap-to-pay capability
65+ year operating history (since 1957) demonstrating stability and member trust
Multiple member testimonials citing exceptional personal service and staff by name
Comprehensive digital banking including online/mobile apps, click pay, and audio response systems
Specialized products like vacation club savings accounts and skip-a-pay loan flexibility
Member-owned, not-for-profit structure returns benefits to members rather than shareholders
cons: Membership restricted to specific employment groups (corrections department), not open to general public
Unsecured loan rates starting at 10.50% APR are higher than some online lenders and banks
Website lacks clear, detailed information about membership eligibility and joining requirements
Limited transparency on account fees, minimum balances, and specific qualification requirements for promotional rates
No information provided about DOCCU's total assets or member count (shown as '0' on website), raising data completeness questions
best_for: Corrections department employees and their immediate families seeking vehicle financing with personalized service
Members prioritizing long-term banking relationships and credit union member-ownership benefits
Borrowers wanting to consolidate debt with flexible repayment options and skip-a-pay features
Individuals seeking cashback rewards on credit card purchases without annual fees
diagnosis: DOCCU is best for corrections department employees and families seeking competitive vehicle and personal loan rates with emphasis on personalized service and long-term member relationships. The primary limitation is membership eligibility restricted to specific employment groups, making it inaccessible to the general public despite offering solid credit union benefits and rates comparable to other NCUA-insured institutions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Department of Corrections (Baton Rouge, LA) | CreditDoc
meta_description: Department of Corrections — NCUA-insured credit union in Baton Rouge, LA. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
